```{r EU FIN IND STXE 600, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("stoxxeur600"))

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Comparison*

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r EU FIN IND ALL, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("dax", "cac40", "ftsemib", "ibex35", "aex", "omx30", "stoxxeur600", "eurostoxx"))

# Compute index
data <- compute_rebase(df = data, base_date = "2015-01-07")

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE, title = "Europe Equity Indices – Indexed Performance (2015:01:07 = 100)"))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### VSTOXX Volatility Index

```{r VSTOXX TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'STOXX — <a href="https://stoxx.com/index/v2tx/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "EURO STOXX 50 Volatility Index measures the market\'s expectation of near-term to long-term volatility in the EURO STOXX 50, maintained by STOXX Ltd. and calculated in real time from the prices of EURO STOXX 50 options across all available strikes and a range of expiration dates. Methodologically, it is computed as the square root of the implied variance derived from the full cross-section of listed options on the EURO STOXX 50, producing a forward-looking estimate of annualized volatility expressed in percentage points, an approach directly analogous to that used by the CBOE VIX for the S&P 500, making the two indices broadly comparable as measures of equity market fear in their respective regions. Because it is anchored to the EURO STOXX 50, the primary blue-chip benchmark for the euro area, the VSTOXX captures investor uncertainty specifically related to European economic conditions, ECB monetary policy, euro area sovereign risk, and geopolitical developments on the continent. Elevated readings historically correspond to periods of financial stress, political uncertainty, or macroeconomic deterioration within the euro area, while low readings reflect investor complacency and stable conditions. It serves as the underlying reference for VSTOXX futures and options traded on Eurex, making it both a sentiment indicator and an actively tradeable volatility benchmark. Quoted in annualized percentage points, updated daily with closing prices")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r VSTOXX, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "vstoxx_idx")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### ZEW Index

```{r ZEW TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Investing — <a href="https://www.investing.com/economic-calendar/zew-economic-sentiment-310" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "Eurozone ZEW Economic Sentiment Index measures the six-month economic outlook for the euro area among financial market experts, published monthly by the Zentrum für Europäische Wirtschaftsforschung (ZEW — Centre for European Economic Research) in Mannheim, Germany. It is derived from a survey of approximately 350 German institutional investors and analysts who assess whether they expect euro area economic conditions to improve, remain unchanged, or deteriorate over the following six months, with the index calculated as the net balance between the share of optimistic and pessimistic respondents. A reading above zero indicates that optimists outnumber pessimists and signals positive near-term growth expectations for the euro area, while a reading below zero reflects a majority expecting deterioration. Because the survey captures the forward-looking expectations of sophisticated financial market participants rather than current business conditions, it serves as a leading indicator of euro area economic activity, with shifts in the index historically anticipating turning points in euro area GDP growth by several months. Updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r ZEW, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "zwe_idx_inv")


# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### Economic Policy Index

```{r EPU TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'EPU — <a href="https://www.policyuncertainty.com/us_monthly.html" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Monthly",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "Economic Policy Uncertainty Indices for Europe, Germany, France, Italy and Spain measure the degree of uncertainty surrounding economic policy across multiple countries and global aggregates, developed by Baker, Bloom, and Davis. Each national index is constructed by combining three components: the frequency of newspaper articles mentioning economic uncertainty and policy-related terms, the number of federal tax code provisions set to expire in coming years, and the dispersion of economic forecasts among professional forecasters, with the relative weighting of components varying by country based on data availability. Elevated readings signal heightened uncertainty about the direction of fiscal, regulatory, monetary, or trade policy, which tends to suppress business investment, hiring, and consumer spending as firms and households delay decisions pending greater clarity, making the indices useful coincident-to-leading indicators of economic activity slowdowns and financial market volatility. All series are reported as diffusion indices normalized to a long-run mean of 100, not seasonally adjusted, released monthly.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r EPU, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("de_epu", "fr_epu", "eu_epu", "es_epu", "it_epu"))

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE, title = "Economic Policy Uncertainty Index for Germany, France, Spain, Italy and Europe"))
div(style = "text-align: center;",
    HTML("<small><em>Note : EU = European Union (27 countries), DE = Germany, FR = France, IT = Italy, ES = Spain </em></small>"))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Indicator  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Indicator

```{=html}
<div class="company-hero">
  <div class="co-body">
    <div class="masthead-title">Indicator</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### KOF Economic Sentiment

```{r KOF ES TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'KOF — <a href="https://kof.ethz.ch/fr/previsions-indicateurs/indicateurs/kof-indice-climat-economique.html" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Monthly",
  adjustment = "Seasonally Adjusted",
  about = "KOF Economic Sentiment Indicator measures the overall economic sentiment in Switzerland, published by the KOF Swiss Economic Institute at ETH Zurich from its business tendency surveys and from consumer surveys conducted by the State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO). It is constructed using the methodology of the European Commission, the same framework applied to all EU member states and euro area aggregates, making it directly comparable to the Economic Sentiment Indicator published for the European Union and the euro area, and thereby allowing Swiss business cycle dynamics to be assessed against those of Switzerland\'s main trading partners on a consistent methodological basis. The composite is assembled from sector-level confidence indicators derived from individual survey responses across five components, weighted as follows: manufacturing (40%), other services (30%), consumer confidence as measured by SECO surveys (20%), construction (5%), and retail trade (5%). The dominant weight assigned to manufacturing reflects its central role in Swiss export activity and GDP, while the inclusion of consumer confidence introduces a demand-side dimension alongside the supply-side business survey components. Reported as a composite index, released monthly.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r KOF ES, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("kof_ind_ch_es", "kof_ind_eu_es"))

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE, title = "KOF ECONOMIC SENTIMENT INDICATOR For switzerland and Europe"))
```

```{=html}
</div>
```

<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Exchange Rates  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Exchange Rates

```{=html}
<div class="company-hero">
  <div class="co-body">
    <div class="masthead-title">Exchange Rates</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### USD/EUR

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r USDEUR, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "eur_usd")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### GBP/EUR

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r GBPEUR, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "gbp_eur")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### CHF/EUR

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r CHFEUR, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "chf_eur")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### CAD/EUR

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r CADEUR, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "cad_eur")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### JPY/EUR

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r JPYEUR, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "jpy_eur")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```
